tipRules.tip: Robertson: Use a 7-inch swimbait on a 1-ounce jighead to target bass holding on offshore hard spots with no visible structure — just a hard bottom detected on electronics.
Robertson: Use a big crankbait like the SPRO Big John when targeting bass offshore on hard-bottom spots in 9-10 feet of water.
Robertson: Pull out big spinnerbaits when fishing offshore hard spots for big bass — complement crankbaits and swimbaits for a multi-lure offshore rotation.

knowledge.insight: Robertson: When idling offshore, look for hard-bottom spots that hold big bass even with no visible structure. A 40x50 yard hard spot in 9-10 feet can hold large concentrations of quality fish.
Robertson: Idle every inch of a lake to find offshore hard spots — if you see big bass on electronics but can't get them to bite in practice, commit to the spot in the tournament.
Robertson: If you're going to fail, fail doing what you do well. Commit to your strengths — for Robertson, that's fishing offshore.
